name: Neighborhood Housing Services of San Antonio
description_short: San Antonio-based nonprofit mortgage lender with 40+ years of experience offering affordable home loans, down payment assistance, and first-time homebuyer education.
description_long:
Neighborhood Housing Services of San Antonio (NHSSA) is a mission-driven nonprofit lender founded over 40 years ago with a focus on making homeownership accessible to families in the San Antonio area and surrounding counties. The organization's stated mission is "Building Successful Families and Communities Through Housing Opportunities." NHSSA is an NMLS-registered lender (NMLS#337024) headquartered at 851 Steves Ave in San Antonio, Texas.

NHSSA offers a comprehensive suite of homeownership services including mortgage loans for home purchase, first-time homebuyer education classes, credit counseling, down payment assistance programs, and property listings for both sale and rent. Their mortgage lending services are complemented by educational preparation to help families qualify and succeed as homeowners. The organization has expanded beyond San Antonio and now serves multiple counties in the region.

The organization distinguishes itself through personalized loan officer support, as evidenced by client testimonials highlighting individual attention and availability beyond standard business hours. NHSSA also builds homes from scratch for clients, creating affordable housing inventory rather than only financing existing properties. Their integration of counseling, education, and lending under one organization creates a holistic pathway to homeownership, rather than treating lending as a standalone transaction.

As a nonprofit focused on affordable housing, NHSSA likely offers rates and terms competitive with or better than market lenders, particularly for first-time homebuyers and lower-income families. However, the website provides limited specific information about loan products, rates, terms, or approval requirements. Prospective borrowers would need direct contact to understand loan options and qualify.
pros: Over 40 years of established experience in affordable housing and homeownership lending
NMLS-regulated mortgage lender with verifiable licensing (NMLS#337024)
Offers integrated homebuyer education and credit counseling alongside mortgage loans
Down payment assistance programs to reduce barrier to entry for first-time buyers
Builds homes from scratch, creating affordable inventory rather than only financing existing properties
Loan officers provide personalized support including evenings and weekends availability
Expanded service area now covers multiple counties beyond San Antonio
cons: Website lacks specific information about loan products, rates, APRs, or terms
No details on debt-to-income requirements, credit score minimums, or loan limits
Limited online application or process information; requires phone contact for details
Website does not specify whether they offer refinancing or only purchase mortgages
No transparency on down payment assistance program eligibility criteria or maximum assistance amounts
best_for: First-time homebuyers in San Antonio and surrounding Texas counties seeking affordable financing
Lower to moderate-income families needing down payment assistance and financial education
Borrowers prioritizing personalized service and nonprofit mission-aligned lending
diagnosis: NHSSA is best for first-time homebuyers and families with moderate-to-low incomes seeking affordable mortgages paired with education and down payment help in the San Antonio region. The main caveat is that the website provides minimal transparency about loan specifics, rates, and eligibility—direct contact via phone (210-533-6673) is necessary to evaluate actual loan options and terms.
services: Homes for sale (NHSSA-built and financed properties)
Multi-county service expansion beyond San Antonio
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
meta_description: San Antonio nonprofit mortgage lender with 40+ years offering affordable home loans, down payment assistance, and first-time homebuyer education.
